title: Smoked Tender French Beef Bourguignon
description: A hearty and comforting French dish featuring tender beef, vine tomatoes, and a rich, smoky flavor. This recipe is a jain-friendly version of the classic Boeuf Bourguignon.
instructions: Heat the oven to 3009 Fahrenheit. Line a large Dutch oven with foil and place the smoked beef short ribs inside.
In a large pan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced red onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the pan and cook until tender, stirring occasionally.
Add the vine tomatoes, beef broth, and thyme to the pan and bring to a simmer.
Remove the pot from the heat and let the ribs smoke for 2 hours, then increase the oven temperature to 3509 Fahrenheit and continue to cook for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the smoked beef bourguignon hot, garnished with fresh parsley, if desired.
Strain the sauce and discard the solids. Add the strained sauce back to the pot with the beef and toss to coat.
Transfer the pot to the oven and cook for an additional 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
Remove the pot from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Slice the beef into thick strips and serve with the smoky sauce spooned over the top.
Strain the sauce and discard the solids. Add the strained sauce back to the pot with the beef and toss to coat.
Transfer the pot to the oven and cook for an additional 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
